info.title: Semrush Analytics API (API key)
info.description:
OpenAPI model for Semrush **API key** access to analytics and related reports.

## Authentication
Use your Semrush **API key** as the query parameter **`key`**. Obtain it from **Subscription info → API units** in the Semrush UI ([quick start](https://developer.semrush.com/api/get-started/quick-start/)). Do not expose keys in public examples.

## Request shape
Almost all Semrush Analytics-style calls are **HTTP GET** to the production host with parameters on the query string. The required parameters depend on the report **`type`** (and product area: SEO, Trends, Projects, Local, etc.).

## Response shape
The **SEO API** returns **CSV** (including a header row) by default. Other Semrush API products may use different formats—check the relevant overview in the developer portal.

## Scope of this document
This file documents the **shared entry point and common parameters** only. Semrush does **not** publish a single OpenAPI file that enumerates every `type` value and column code; use the linked product docs for complete method lists, pricing in API units, filters, and error semantics.

## Related
For **App Center** server-to-server (JWT exchange + Hermes) endpoints, see `custom.semrush.json` in this repository.

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: Reports invoked with `type` and other query parameters (SEO, Trends, Projects, Local, etc.).

get.description:
Call the Semrush API with your **`key`** and a report **`type`**, plus any parameters required for that report.

Examples:
- [SEO API overview & filters](https://developer.semrush.com/api/seo/overview/)
- [SEO overview reports](https://developer.semrush.com/api/seo/overview-reports/)
- [Trends API](https://developer.semrush.com/api/trends/overview/)
- [Projects API](https://developer.semrush.com/api/projects/overview/)
- [Local API](https://developer.semrush.com/api/local/overview/)

Logical or validation errors may still return **HTTP 200** with an `ERROR` line in the body for some report types—always inspect the payload.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Report / method identifier (e.g. `domain_ranks`, `domain_organic`, `phrase_related`). Allowed values and required companion parameters are defined per product in the Semrush API documentation.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Target domain for domain-centric reports (when required by `type`).

parameters.name: phrase
parameters.description: Target phrase for keyword-centric reports (when required by `type`).

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Target page URL for URL-centric reports (when required by `type`).

parameters.name: database
parameters.description: Regional database code (e.g. `us`, `uk`, or mobile / extended variants per docs).

parameters.name: export_columns
parameters.description: Comma-separated column codes for the response (SEO API and others—see each method’s column table).

parameters.name: display_limit
parameters.description: Limits how many data rows are returned to conserve [API units](https://developer.semrush.com/api/get-started/api-units-balance/).

parameters.name: display_sort
parameters.description: Sort order for results (e.g. `nq_desc`, `tr_desc`) where supported—typically one rule per request.

parameters.name: display_filter
parameters.description: URL-encoded filter pipeline (SEO API). Use `%7C` for `|` between filter parts; max 25 filters.

200.description: Report data. SEO API responses are usually **CSV** (semicolon- or comma-separated depending on export settings). Some products may return other media types.

schema.description: CSV or plain-text report body. May contain an ERROR line on success status for some calls.

400.description: Malformed request or unsupported combination of parameters.

401.description: Authentication failed (invalid or missing API key), if returned for this product.

429.description: Rate limiting or usage restrictions exceeded. See [API usage restrictions](https://developer.semrush.com/api/introduction/api-usage-restrictions).

ApiKeyQuery.name: key
ApiKeyQuery.description: Semrush API key (same as `key=` in official examples). Configure in Semrush under Subscription info → API units.
